items.rationale: The house's single highest-conviction theme: stablecoins have achieved durable product-market fit, are the cheapest way to move a dollar (<1s, <1¢), rival Visa/ACH in volume, are uncorrelated with crypto trading (organic use), and reinforce USD dominance via Treasury holdings. GENIUS Act gave them legal clarity.
items.age_note: Building since 2024-08 ('killer app'); intensified and confirmed through 2025-10

citations.quote: Stablecoins have found product-market fit, now rivaling the world's largest payment networks in transaction volume.
Stablecoins now present what I believe is the first credible opportunity to onboard a billion people into crypto.
Stablecoin activity is largely uncorrelated to crypto market cycles – organic, non-speculative use appears to be widespread and growing.

items.rationale: AI agents can't have bank accounts but can have crypto wallets; legacy rails reject machine-speed automated payments. The team sees crypto as possibly the only viable payment layer for autonomous agents, and micropayments finally becoming a real business model.
items.age_note: Idea since 2024-09 ('AI agents can have crypto wallets'); investment-backed and accelerating through 2025-09/10

citations.quote: Crypto might be the only viable payment rail for AI agents. Will be an exciting category to watch.
x402 means micropayments might finally be a real business model online
AI agents are becoming more than just tools... our current financial infrastructure wasn't built for them.

items.rationale: Eddy Lazzarin's signature thesis: crypto networks are the native antidote to physical-infrastructure monopolies, letting the physical world be dominated by user-generated services ('atoms look like bits'). Verification of physical (non-computational) state is the defining hard problem.
items.age_note: Consistent 2024-06 through 2025-06; definition refined over time but conviction persisted

citations.quote: DePIN is eating the world
DePIN provides the most salient path towards making atoms look more like bits for builders and investors alike.

items.rationale: Reframed from moral/commercial 'nice-to-have' to a critical bottleneck and potential dealbreaker for institutional adoption. As crypto goes mainstream, financial privacy is expected; demand is explicitly rising. TEEs seen as becoming a default ('HTTPS vs HTTP').
items.age_note: Escalated sharply in 2025; Sam Broner now calls lack of privacy a near-dealbreaker (2025-09)

citations.quote: Demand for privacy is on the rise.
the lack of privacy onchain is one of the biggest sources of friction, if not a dealbreaker, for many institutions. It's critical.

items.rationale: Framed via Hayek's knowledge problem — markets as information systems that outperform polls and experts. Co-led Kalshi Series D; sees prediction markets as a trust-earning alternative to failing institutions, alongside DeFi and stablecoins.
items.age_note: Long-running interest (2024 election-market commentary); converted to capital commitment 2025-10

citations.quote: the answer to the knowledge problem is not to give central planners more sophisticated computers... markets themselves are the computers.
New types of applications — like perps, prediction markets, and RWA tokenization — broke out in 2025.

items.rationale: 2025 declared 'the year of institutional adoption' — banks, asset managers, fintechs making real commitments; $175B in crypto ETFs, Circle IPO, $30B RWAs tokenized. Seen as a maturity inflection unlike prior narrative-driven cycles.
items.age_note: New dominant 2025 theme; crystallized in 2025-10 State of Crypto report

citations.quote: Some of the largest financial institutions aren't just exploring crypto, they're making real commitments.
Crypto has exited the echo chamber and entered the enterprise.

items.rationale: Growing share of activity (20% of spot trading onchain), now seen as defensible 'neutral infrastructure.' Strong recent emphasis that DeFi must be protected in legislation and not regulated as intermediaries.
items.age_note: Sustained; legal-protection urgency rose in 2025

citations.quote: 20% of spot crypto trading now happens in DeFi
Market structure can't work without protecting DeFi.

items.rationale: Justin Thaler's structural bet: as prover overhead falls, applicable SNARK use cases grow dramatically; Jolt's sum-check + lookups approach seen as state-of-the-art. Verifiable compute as a new developer primitive for private DeFi, onchain ML, and decentralized AI.
items.age_note: Tech-excitement persistent 2024-05 → 2025-08; tempered by explicit not-production-ready caveats (see concerns)

citations.quote: I still think ZK is under hyped.
Fast provers need the sum-check protocol and multilinear polynomials, not univariate polynomials

items.rationale: Generative AI is breaking the creator-platform covenant; blockchains can provide an open, programmable IP/provenance and licensing layer so creators get compensated. Backed Story ($80M) and Poseidon (AI training-data coordination).
items.age_note: Consistent since 2024-08; reinforced 2025-07 with Poseidon

citations.quote: We want to help Story become the infrastructure for AI systems and creative people to happily co-exist.
data has become a limiting factor in AI progress... A decentralized approach can solve this.

items.rationale: Miles Jennings repeatedly warns these are 'ownership of nothing,' fickle, harm consumers, and risk turning network-token markets into the inefficient ICO/SPAC pattern. Pure speculative demand detached from economic value crowds out real value creation.
items.age_note: Consistent 2024-09 → 2025-04

citations.quote: Memecoins are the opposite of equity — ownership of nothing versus something.
pure speculative interest detached from any economic value has proven to be fickle, exposing consumers to significant harms.

items.rationale: Central legal worry: tokens whose value depends on a controlling team/company (FTT archetype) carry securities-like trust dependencies and information asymmetry; conflating them with true network tokens would create a loophole that damages capital markets.
items.age_note: Intensified through 2025 with the 7-token taxonomy work

citations.quote: A token that does both is just a security. ETH holders do not own Consensys.
calls for market structure legislation that ignore decentralization are calls to create a significant loophole in securities laws

items.rationale: Despite optimism, the team fears legislation that punts to regulators, undermines CLARITY's transfer-restriction/decentralization framework, or tries to regulate autonomous protocols — which would force intermediaries back in and 'kill the technology.'
items.age_note: Current as of 2025-08/10; replaced earlier 2024 anti-SEC-lawfare framing now that posture has improved

citations.quote: Regulating autonomous blockchain protocols is not a viable solution — it would kill the technology.
The Senate's approach is simple because it punts all the hard stuff to regulators.

items.rationale: Justin Thaler's explicit caution that despite promise, zkVMs are riddled with bugs, formal verification is years off, and overheads exceed 100,000× — dangerous to deploy in high-stakes settings; security, not performance, is the biggest threat to SNARKs' potential.
items.age_note: Consistent 2024-05 → 2025-03; a long-standing internal counterweight to ZK hype

citations.quote: while SNARKs and zkVMs show immense promise, they're not ready for complex, high-stakes deployments. Bugs are everywhere
they need to be secure they're not now they're riddled with bugs

items.rationale: Research-driven concern: PBS decentralizes validation but centralizes block building ('proof of MEV' feedback loop, big-get-bigger); multi-block and cross-chain MEV could make it much worse. Order-flow advantages risk monopolizing the building market.
items.age_note: Detailed in 2024-10 research sessions; not heavily restated since but unrebutted

citations.quote: PBS is reducing centralization in validation, but might create centralization in building because of this... MEV competition

items.rationale: Newly surfaced structural risk: ~$750B of Bitcoin sits in quantum-vulnerable addresses; chains are starting to think about migrating to quantum-secure cryptography even though the timeline is unclear.
items.age_note: Emerging late-2025 concern (2025-10)

citations.quote: Today, $750 billion worth of Bitcoin sits in quantum vulnerable addresses.

items.rationale: Miles flags unsolved problems: PubCo disclosure obligations conflict with securities-risk for the underlying asset, Gary Plastic issues, and trading below NAV — manageable for BTC/ETH/SOL but risky when teams spin up vehicles for their own immature tokens.
items.age_note: Raised 2025-07; specific and current

citations.quote: I'm not sure these problems are solvable where management teams of less mature projects are spinning up and controlling these public vehicles themselves.

items.rationale: Macro framing for why decentralization matters now: AI compute, infrastructure, and revenue have concentrated in the 'Magnificent 7,' which are the only real source of US economic growth — a centralization the house wants crypto to counterbalance.
items.age_note: Stated 2025-10-31, very current

citations.quote: The compute resources AI is trained with, the infrastructure AI runs on, and the revenue that AI generates has consolidated into a handful of big companies.

items.rationale: The canonical 'network token': value accrues via the EIP-1559 burn (a fully automatic buyback) and protocol fees, expected to be deflationary long-term; held up as the model for a token tied to a system that operates without human control.
items.age_note: Consistent 2024-09 → 2025-09

citations.quote: The value accrues to ETH through the burn.
long term I think most of us expect eth to be a deflationary currency

items.rationale: Praised for 'shipping beats maximalism,' pragmatic engineering, and becoming a dominant DeFi venue / 'Internet Capital Markets' platform; a16z invested in Jito and partnered to push Solana further. Solana drives much recent active-address and stablecoin growth.
items.age_note: Warmed notably through 2025; capital-backed 2025-10

citations.quote: There are no zealots in Solana, only pragmatic engineers... shipping beats maximalism
help Solana become the dominant venue for Internet Capital Markets.

items.rationale: Direct token investment + partnership (2025-10) to address insidious MEV on Solana via the Block Assembly Marketplace (encrypted-until-execution + app-defined ordering), enabling onchain order books and dark pools.
items.age_note: New conviction, 2025-10

citations.quote: Jito's goal with their latest innovation—called the Block Assembly Marketplace (BAM)—is to address some of the more insidious forms of MEV.

items.rationale: Added $70M in EIGEN with lockup; sees Eigen unlocking a verifiable, credibly-neutral application paradigm and infrastructure AI agents will need to interact with crypto-based finance.
items.age_note: Conviction grew through 2025; reinforced 2025-06/09

citations.quote: the Eigen team has unlocked a whole new paradigm for building applications that are verifiable and credibly neutral.

items.rationale: Invested an additional $55M in ZRO with a 3-year lockup; values the protocol's role enabling businesses and complex workflows to move onchain, and praised its Sybil-filtering of airdrops.
items.age_note: Position taken 2025-04; not restated recently (persisted)

citations.quote: we've invested an additional $55M in @LayerZero_Core tokens (ZRO), with a 3-year lockup.

items.rationale: Used as the clean example of a network token that can capture protocol fees (via UniStaker) without being a security; Uniswap Foundation held up as the model decentralized ecosystem steward.
items.age_note: Cited as exemplar through 2025; illustrative more than a directional bet

citations.quote: UNI can capture fees from the network (the switch is off right now) via a mechanism like UniStaker.

items.rationale: Respected as the most secure, decentralized network and a legitimate (non-meme) store of value; 'incredible that we have bitcoin.' Excitement is qualified by emerging quantum-address vulnerability and skepticism toward a strategic reserve as a priority.
items.age_note: Positive but tempered; reserve-priority skepticism 2024-11, quantum caveat 2025-10

citations.quote: being the native asset of what is arguably the most secure and decentralized network gives it economic characteristics... unlike any memecoin.
It's incredible that we have bitcoin.

items.rationale: Treated as speculative vehicles with no inherent value; the team warns that brand-attached or insider-heavy launches (rumored ~90% insider supply in some cases) condition the market for insiders to dump on retail, and that such launches should still face Howey.
items.age_note: 2025-01 → 2025-04; recurring caution

citations.quote: if the team held 90% of the supply (as rumored), then they very much wouldn't have qualified under the test we've proposed
What's the purpose of using a company's brand for a memecoin?

items.rationale: The recurring cautionary archetype: economically a synthetic profits-interest in a centralized company (FTX) despite no legal rights — would go to zero if the company shut down, and should be regulated as a security. Used to define the category a16z wants the market to avoid.
items.age_note: Used as canonical negative example throughout 2024-2025 (token itself defunct)

citations.quote: FTT was a security. If you have an argument as to why it wasn't, I'm all ears.
the token would go to zero... a profits interest in the company — its risk profile would look identical to that of a security.

items.rationale: Despite excitement about decentralized algorithmic stablecoins as an underexplored model, the house signals strong aversion to LUNA-style designs and emphasizes interest-bearing or risk-juicing structures that undermine the dollar-equivalent risk profile.
items.age_note: 2025-02 / 2025-03; cautionary aside within a broadly pro-stablecoin stance

citations.quote: I expect more experiments here (no LUNA please).
Allowing interest payments would incentivize issuers to take more risk to juice returns, thereby undermining the safety of the stablecoin.

risk_regime.rationale: As of late October 2025 the house is at peak structural optimism: GENIUS Act signed into law, CLARITY advancing with broad bipartisan support, crypto market cap >$4T, real institutional adoption, stablecoin PMF, reliable infra (~3,400 TPS), and a friendlier SEC issuing no-action relief. Leadership explicitly says it has 'never been a better time to start a project in crypto' and is 'more optimistic than ever.' The risk-on tilt is qualified — not euphoric — by genuine concerns about legislative execution (DeFi protections, Senate draft), speculation/memecoins, zkVM security, MEV centralization, and emerging quantum risk; the team frames caution as 'cynicism is self-defeating' and urges continued building.

notes: Corpus is multi-author; views were attributed to their dominant voices and weighted by recency. Key reversal/evolution: regulatory posture flipped from defensive anti-SEC 'lawfare' framing (2024) to offense — building constructive legislation (GENIUS/CLARITY) by 2025. Foundation structures went from standard practice to explicitly discouraged ('the foundation era is over,' 2025-06). 'Decentralization' was redefined house-wide from the SEC's 2019 efforts-based test to a control-based test, with Eddy Lazzarin even arguing (2025-07) to drop the word 'decentralization' for concrete goals (censorship resistance, composability, no platform risk). A persistent (not recently restated) research concern is MEV/builder centralization (2024-10). The token taxonomy work (network vs. company-backed vs. arcade vs. memecoin) is the central analytical lens and the source of most token-level concern. Strongest single conviction across the whole team is stablecoins; fastest-rising are AI-agent payments and onchain privacy. Distinguish house 'excitement' (sectors/theses) from portfolio positions (JTO, EIGEN, ZRO, Story, Kalshi, EigenCloud) — the latter are capital-backed convictions, not neutral market reads.
